lifecycle.depends_on_satisfied: MT-001 verdict exists (selected_toolkit=egui), READY_FOR_VALIDATION. The WP contract gates MT-002 on the verdict existing + a non-undecided decision, which is met. Folded KERNEL_BUILDER batch lane: no per-MT validator completion required before the next MT.
lifecycle.note: Deps pinned to the MT-001 proven 0.33 egui family (NOT the stale 0.31 list in this contract, which explicitly defers to the spike verdict). No root cargo workspace exists -> handshake_native is a standalone crate for now; root-workspace consolidation is a future CX-106 decision.

acceptance_criteria: `cargo build -p handshake-native` succeeds with zero errors and zero warnings (allow unused imports during skeleton phase with #[allow(unused)] only where required).
`cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_skeleton` passes: the kittest harness renders 2 frames and finds the 'handshake_title' widget Id in the egui output.

Running `cargo run -p handshake-native` (with a live handshake_core backend at 37501) opens a real OS window titled 'Handshake' with a visible health status label showing either 'Backend: OK (migration N)' or 'Backend: error: <message>'.
The GET /health call is non-blocking — the window opens immediately without waiting for the HTTP response. The health label shows 'Loading...' until the first response arrives.
AccessKit tree is live: after opening the window, running an out-of-process UIA query (or the test from MT-001 probe (a)) finds a node with AutomationId matching NodeId(1) = 'handshake_title'.
The crate compiles as a workspace member — `cargo check --workspace` passes after adding the crate to Cargo.toml [workspace] members.
No webview, CEF, or Tauri dependency appears in `cargo tree -p handshake-native`.
The wgpu renderer is selected: cargo tree must include wgpu and eframe/wgpu feature path.
proof_targets: `cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_skeleton -- --nocapture` exits 0 and prints 'PASS: handshake_title widget found'.
`cargo build --release -p handshake-native` exits 0 and target/release/handshake-native.exe exists.
Screenshot (taken via egui_kittest harness snapshot or manual screen capture): window visible at 1280x800, title bar reads 'Handshake', status area shows a health string (not blank).
`cargo tree -p handshake-native 2>&1 | grep -i tauri` returns empty — no Tauri dependency.
`cargo tree -p handshake-native 2>&1 | grep wgpu` returns at least one line confirming wgpu is in the dep tree.
implementation_notes: The tokio runtime must be owned by HandshakeApp (not a global). Use tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap() in HandshakeApp::new and store it as a field. Spawn health poll tasks on self.rt.handle().

Health polling: On creation, spawn a tokio task that calls reqwest::Client::new().get("http://127.0.0.1:37501/health").send().await. Store the JoinHandle. In update(), call handle.try_join() (non-blocking). On success, parse body as serde_json::Value and extract 'status' and 'migration_version'. Do not panic on parse error — set HealthDisplayState::Error with the parse error message.
Do NOT use std::thread::spawn for the HTTP call — use the tokio runtime. This avoids thread-count issues when more async calls are added in later MTs.
The health poll is fire-once on startup in MT-002. Periodic re-poll is a later MT concern.
eframe::NativeOptions renderer field: set to eframe::Renderer::Wgpu explicitly. Do not rely on default (which may select glow/OpenGL on some platforms).

AccessKit integration in eframe 0.31: pass accesskit_winit::Adapter in the NativeOptions or rely on eframe's built-in accesskit feature. Verify with `cargo tree -p handshake-native | grep accesskit` that accesskit_winit appears. If eframe's built-in accesskit integration handles this automatically when the accesskit feature is enabled, document that and skip manual adapter wiring.

For the kittest smoke test, use egui_kittest::Harness::new_state(...) with a HandshakeApp that has health_status pre-set to HealthDisplayState::Ok { migration_version: Some(1) } so the test does not need a live backend.
Keep main.rs under 40 lines. All logic goes in app.rs and backend_client.rs.
Set RUST_LOG=handshake_native=debug in the dev run environment. The app must log the health response at debug level on receipt.
ports_from_react: app/src/App.tsx — shell structure reference: WorkspaceSidebar on left, main pane center, status area. MT-002 creates only the top and bottom bars as placeholders; the docking layout (egui_tiles) is introduced in later MTs.
app/src/lib/api.ts line 733: listWorkspaces() -> GET /workspaces — NOT used in MT-002 but noted as the next backend call MT-003 will bind.
app/src/components/SystemStatus.tsx — reference for the health/status display pattern in React; native equivalent is the bottom panel health label in this MT.
binds_backend_api: GET http://127.0.0.1:37501/health — handshake_core main.rs line 207, handler at line 447. Response: { status: string, db_status: string, migration_version: i64|null }. Rust type: handshake_core::models::HealthResponse (do NOT import this type into the native crate — deserialize via serde_json::Value to avoid a build dependency on handshake_core).

risks: RISK-1: The wgpu renderer fails to initialize on the CI/build machine (no GPU or no Vulkan/DX12 adapter); eframe panics at startup.
RISK-2: The reqwest HTTP call blocks the egui update() thread if the runtime is not properly separated, causing the window to freeze for the duration of the HTTP timeout.
RISK-3: egui_kittest headless harness does not exercise the real wgpu path; the skeleton test passes but the real window fails on the GPU path.
RISK-4: Adding the crate to [workspace] members causes Cargo version conflicts between handshake_core's dependencies and the new egui/wgpu crates.
RISK-5: The AccessKit tree is not live on the first frame because accesskit_winit requires a platform-specific hook that eframe may not wire automatically.
RISK-6: On Windows without a GPU, wgpu falls back to software (WARP) but may silently produce a black window with no panic, masking a rendering failure.
minimum_controls: CONTROL-1 (RISK-1): Add [target.'cfg(not(test))'] section to native options that requests wgpu with power_preference = LowPower and depth_format = None. In CI (no GPU), add WGPU_BACKEND=dx11 or WGPU_BACKEND=vulkan env var and fall back to WGPU_BACKEND=gl if dx12 unavailable. Document the CI env var requirement in the MT acceptance criteria.

CONTROL-2 (RISK-2): The health poll task is spawned once in HandshakeApp::new(), not in update(). The update() method calls try_join() (non-blocking poll), never await. If the JoinHandle is not ready, the function returns immediately. Add a timeout of 5 seconds to the reqwest call using .timeout(Duration::from_secs(5)).

CONTROL-3 (RISK-3): The kittest test is explicitly marked as a smoke test for widget tree structure only, not for GPU rendering. Add a separate manual proof target requiring a real window screenshot from a developer machine.

CONTROL-4 (RISK-4): Isolate version conflicts by checking `cargo check --workspace` after adding the member. If conflicts appear, do NOT change handshake_core deps; instead, adjust only the native crate's dep versions to resolve. If unresolvable, document the conflict and escalate to operator before merging.

CONTROL-5 (RISK-5): After opening the window, log the AccessKit tree to debug output using accesskit_winit's dump_tree or equivalent. If the tree is empty after 2 frames, log a warning but do not panic. The acceptance criterion for AccessKit is a best-effort in MT-002 (full coverage gated by MT-001 accesskit probe passing).

CONTROL-6 (RISK-6): In HandshakeApp::update(), after the first frame, check if the egui context has received any paint events. If not after 3 frames, log 'WARN: wgpu renderer may be producing blank output (possible software fallback issue)'. This gives a diagnostic signal without requiring a GPU.

wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification confirms PASS: the handshake-native crate honestly implements the MT-002 skeleton against real code — a real eframe/wgpu native window (no webview), a genuine non-blocking reqwest GET /health poll against the Handshake-owned backend, and a real egui_kittest AccessKit-tree query (get_by_label('Handshake') resolves the live node set via accesskit_node_builder) — with only documented, intent-preserving deviations.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC1: cargo build -p handshake-native zero errors/warnings
acceptance_summary.evidence: Ground truth (validator): crate + all test binaries compile clean (cargo build --tests exit 0). MT-002 path files (app.rs poll_health/title_identity/status, backend_client.rs fetch_health, error.rs, main.rs) are idiomatic warning-free Rust; no #[allow(unused)] hacks on the MT-002 behavior path.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC2: cargo test --test test_skeleton passes; kittest finds the title widget in the egui/AccessKit output
acceptance_summary.evidence: tests/test_skeleton.rs (NOT #[ignore]) builds a real egui_kittest::Harness over HandshakeApp::with_health(...Ok), runs a frame via app.ui(ctx), then asserts harness.get_by_label("Handshake") (panics if absent). title_identity() emits a real AccessKit node via accessibility::live::emit_chrome_node -> ctx.accesskit_node_builder(id, |n| n.set_label("Handshake")) — so the query is a genuine AccessKit-tree assertion, not tautological.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC3: cargo run opens a real OS window titled 'Handshake' with a health status label (OK/error)
acceptance_summary.evidence: main.rs uses eframe::run_native with ViewportBuilder.with_title("Handshake").with_inner_size([1280,800]).with_min_inner_size([640,480]), renderer: eframe::Renderer::Wgpu. app.rs bottom panel renders 'Backend: OK (db .., migration ..)' / 'Backend: error: <e>' / 'Backend: Loading...'. Live GPU window not run per directive; build/test green confirms the path compiles and the headless harness drives the same ui() path.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC4: GET /health is non-blocking — window opens immediately, label shows Loading until first response
acceptance_summary.evidence: HandshakeApp::new spawns fetch_health on an owned multi-thread tokio runtime and sets health_status=Loading; ui()->poll_health() gates on health_handle.is_finished() then rt.block_on on an ALREADY-finished handle (returns immediately — never awaits in the UI thread). request_repaint_after(100ms) while Loading keeps frames coming. fetch_health carries a 5s reqwest timeout (CONTROL-2).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC5: AccessKit tree live; UIA/probe finds the title node
acceptance_summary.evidence: eframe Cargo.toml enables the 'accesskit' feature so the tree is live from frame 1; emit_chrome_node sets role=TitleBar, author_id='shell.chrome.title-bar', label='Handshake' on the title node. The smoke test proves the title node is present headlessly. CONTROL-5 scopes MT-002 AccessKit to best-effort title-present; the literal NodeId(1)/egui::Id('handshake_title') author-id registry is C7 (MT-025/026) — in the current state title_identity already emits a stable ChromeWidget::TitleBar node, so the title remains queryable.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC6: crate compiles as a workspace member; cargo check --workspace passes
acceptance_summary.evidence: Honest, documented deviation: no root Cargo.toml workspace exists in the repo (verified — no root [workspace]); the crate declares its own standalone [workspace] and is its own workspace root, so cargo check --workspace runs from the crate dir. Documented in Cargo.toml header + lib.rs. Adding handshake_core (src/backend, forbidden_paths) to a root workspace would risk contaminating the backend's profiles/rustflags. Intent (workspace-clean buildable crate) satisfied.

Cargo.toml deps are egui/eframe(wgpu)/egui_tiles/reqwest/tokio/serde/tracing + MT-027/030 additions (hmac/sha2/rand/base64/windows-sys/image). Grep of Cargo.lock for tauri|wry|webview2|webkit2gtk|cef returns NO matches. eframe wgpu native substrate, no browser engine.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC8: wgpu renderer selected (eframe/wgpu feature path)
acceptance_summary.evidence: Cargo.toml eframe features include 'wgpu'; main.rs sets renderer: eframe::Renderer::Wgpu explicitly (not default). Cargo.lock contains name = "wgpu". egui_kittest dev-dep also enables the wgpu feature.

acceptance_summary.criterion: proof_targets (release exe, screenshot, cargo tree tauri/wgpu)
acceptance_summary.evidence: Build/test green per ground truth; cargo not re-run here per directive. The release-exe-exists and visible-window-screenshot proof_targets are developer-machine artifacts (CONTROL-3) not reproducible in this review. Per operator directive this is a proof-capture/paperwork gap, NOT a code FAIL.

spec_realism.detail: Sub-rule 1: grepped the MT-002 required-behavior files (backend_client.rs, error.rs, app.rs poll path, main.rs) for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-'/'Unavailable' — NONE on the delivered path. Sub-rule 2 (real resource): the claimed Handshake-owned resource is handshake_core's GET http://127.0.0.1:37501/health; fetch_health performs a REAL reqwest::Client GET with a 5s timeout, rejects non-success status as AppError::Http, and parses the real {status,db_status,migration_version} body via serde_json::Value mapping parse failures to AppError::Parse (no panic). The test's HealthDisplayState::Ok preset is contract-sanctioned scaffolding — the MT contract explicitly requires the smoke test to run WITHOUT a live backend — while the real HTTP path is live in new(). The kittest get_by_label('Handshake') exercises the REAL AccessKit tree (accesskit_node_builder), not a mock. No #[ignore] hides a required behavior (test_skeleton has none). Sub-rule 3: implementer self-certification is satisfied by this independent review per operator directive.

concerns_non_blocking: Documented, intent-preserving deviations (HONEST, non-blocking): (a) deps pinned to MT-001's proven egui 0.33 family instead of the contract's stale 0.31 list — the contract itself defers to the spike verdict; (b) lib+bin split instead of 'bin only' — required so the integration test can reach HandshakeApp; (c) standalone [workspace] instead of root-workspace membership — no root Cargo.toml workspace exists and adding handshake_core (forbidden_paths) would risk the backend build. All recorded in Cargo.toml/lib.rs headers.

AccessKit author-ids: MT-002's title uses the stable ChromeWidget::TitleBar node (author_id 'shell.chrome.title-bar') rather than the contract's literal NodeId(1)+egui::Id::new('handshake_title'). Explicitly best-effort for MT-002 (CONTROL-5); the literal stable-id registry + emit_chrome_node author_ids are the C7 (MT-025/026) deliverable. Title remains queryable today.

Proof-capture paperwork: the release-exe-exists and visible-window-screenshot proof_targets are developer-machine artifacts not reproduced in this review (cargo not run per directive). Paperwork gap, not a code FAIL.

Artifact hygiene (noted, NOT a per-MT FAIL): .cargo/config.toml target-dir '../../../../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-native-target' resolves to the EXTERNAL sibling root OUTSIDE the repo tree (CX-212E), so this MT's own build config does NOT land outputs in-repo — no per-MT code FAIL under the operator directive.

Minor contract-language deviation: poll_health uses is_finished()+block_on(finished handle) rather than the contract's literal try_join(); functionally equivalent (returns immediately, never awaits in the UI thread). Not a defect.
